title: Key Takeaways from the 2026 Oscars: One Battle After Another's Victory
text: Paul Thomas Anderson's political thriller dominated the 98th Academy Awards, securing six Oscars from 13 nominations. One Battle After Another faced strong competition from the vampire film Sinners, which arrived with a record 16 nominations. Anderson prevailed in several top categories, including Best Picture and Best Director, while both filmmakers earned writing awards: Anderson for Best Adapted Screenplay and director Ryan Coogler for Best Original Screenplay. Michael B. Jordan claimed Best Actor, defeating Marty Supreme star Timothée Chalamet in a competitive race. The hosts made subtle jabs at Chalamet's recent comments disparaging opera and ballet. Cinematographer Autumn Durald Arkapaw made history as the first woman to win Best Cinematography on her first nomination. Several critically acclaimed films were shut out entirely, including Marty Supreme with nine nominations but zero wins, and Yorgos Lanthimos's Bugonia. Host Conan O'Brien and presenter Jimmy Kimmel made veiled criticisms of President Trump's policies without naming him directly. Several honorees referenced ongoing conflicts and authoritarianism, though most kept remarks diplomatically vague, emphasizing cinema's universal power to transcend borders.
